fact_nuggets.text: Snowfall redirects here. For the location in Pokemon Channel, see Mt. Snowfall. Snow Warning (Japanese: Snowfall) is an Ability introduced in Generation IV. Prior to Generation VI, it was the signature Ability of Snover and Abomasnow.
Signature Ability of Snover And Abomasnow
Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description MD T D S Changes the weather to a hail condition. B S L MD GtI Unavailable S MD The weather on the floor will be hail!

Contents 1 Effect 1.1 In battle 1.1.1 Generations IV and V 1.1.2 Generation VI to VIII 1.1.3 Generation IX 1.2 Outside of battle Effect [ edit | edit source ] In battle [ edit | edit source ] Generations IV and V [ edit | edit source ] Snow Warning summons a hailstorm in battle as soon as a Pokemon with Snow Warning enters the battle. Its effects are the same as Hail , but instead of lasting for five turns, it lasts for the whole battle until replaced. If a Pokemon with Snow Warning is sent out in the same turn as another Pokemon whose Ability affects the weather , the slower Pokemon's Ability will override the faster Ability. Generation VI to VIII [ edit | edit source ] The hail will last only for five turns. This can be extended to eight turns if the Pokemon is holding an Icy Rock . If heavy rain , extremely harsh sunlight , or strong winds are present, Snow Warning will fail to activate. Generation IX [ edit | edit source ] Snow Warning now summons a snowstorm instead of hail. Outside of battle [ edit | edit source ] Snow Warning has no effect outside of battle.

Summons hail that lasts indefinitely upon entering battle.
